title: Apply SHELL during image build time
body:
# Overview
This PR fixes the behaviour of `SHELL` by making sure that it applies during image build time in addition to existing functionality. Resolves #1581.

# Testing
4 new tests added:
- default shell + OCI format
- default shell + Docker format
- custom shell + OCI format
- custom shell + Docker format

Output:
```
 ✓ bud-shell during build in Docker format
 ✓ bud-shell during build in OCI format
 ✓ bud-shell changed during build in Docker format
 ✓ bud-shell changed during build in OCI format
...
296 tests, 10 failures, 5 skipped
```
10 tests that are failing (as far as I can see) rely on an image registry being setup at `localhost`. Therefore, I don't think they're caused by my changes.

# Signing
Signed-off-by: Eric Hripko <ehripko@bloomberg.net>

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`

Thanks @EricHripko Looks great 
LGTM
Could you check if this works properly if you do this via Bash instead of Dockerfile?

```
# ctr=$(buildah from --format docker fedora)
#  buildah config --shell '/bin/sh -c' $ctr 
#  buildah run $ctr  echo "SHELL=$0"
```

Hi @rhatdan! Thank you for looking at this :+1: 

I tried to get this to work, but I am struggling with some shell expansion. Namely, I get the following:
```
   #|     FAIL: buildah --debug=false run ubuntu-working-container echo SHELL=/usr/lib/bats/bats-exec-test
   #| expected: 'SHELL=/bin/bash'
   #|   actual: 'SHELL=/usr/lib/bats/bats-exec-test
```
It seems that host shell insists on expanding `$0` before it gets inside a working container:
```
$ buildah run ubuntu-working-container echo "SHELL=$0"
SHELL=bash
# ^- should be /bin/sh
```
I've tried various escaping tricks, but it seems like the only 'proper' way to do this is to do something like `buildah run ubuntu-working-container sh -c 'echo SHELL=$0'`. Unfortunately, at this point we are no longer testing the default shell :slightly_frowning_face:

Do you have any suggestions on how to get this working?

I can not get it to work either.  But how about this patch, which I believe will cover both use cases.

https://paste.fedoraproject.org/paste/~To3nUps4Ti4KuzHKkthTA

With my patch I am getting.
```
# ./buildah config --shell 'bash -c' $ctr 
# ./buildah run $ctr  'echo $0'
/usr/bin/bash
# ./buildah config --shell 'sh -c' $ctr 
# ./buildah run $ctr  'echo $0'
/usr/bin/sh
```

With this patch, we would be always forcing the shell to take effect. This means that cases below would use the shell whilst they didn't previously. 
- `RUN ["echo", "stuff"]`
- `buildah run $ctr echo "stuff"`
  ( ^- guess that's why we couldn't get the test going, the code path doesn't allow it currently :see_no_evil: )

This would be the case for both custom and default shell - and for both Docker and OCI formats too.
Is this ok?
Well could you check if we match Docker behaviour?

Shell is indeed not used for JSON format of the `RUN` instruction. As a result, `$0` isn't expanded at all in Docker:
```
Step 1/3 : FROM ubuntu
 ---> 94e814e2efa8
Step 2/3 : RUN echo $0
 ---> Running in 9ab2d72cf9c3
/bin/sh
Removing intermediate container 9ab2d72cf9c3
 ---> 1246e0ad732b
Step 3/3 : RUN ["echo", "$0"]
 ---> Running in ba2508344e4a
$0
Removing intermediate container ba2508344e4a
 ---> 5fe7ffc812ef
Successfully built 5fe7ffc812ef
```

Shell also doesn't appear to be used for the second case. This makes the current behaviour (without your patch) of `buildah run` match `docker run`:
```
$ docker run --rm ubuntu bash -c 'echo $0'
bash
$ docker run --rm ubuntu sh -c 'echo $0'
sh
```

My goal here is to make 
buildah run work just like the RUN directive in the Dockerfile.
`docker run` and `podman run` are much different things.

In a Dockerfile, does docker handle
RUN echo $0
versus
RUN [ "echo", "$0" ]

Sorry, I initially misunderstood your question.
Yes, `RUN echo $0` correctly prints the current shell.

__Default shell:__
```
...
Step 2/2 : RUN echo $0
 ---> Running in 94f4a2336321
/bin/sh
Removing intermediate container 94f4a2336321
 ---> c87a94c5de69
Successfully built c87a94c5de69
```

__Custom shell:__
```
Step 2/3 : SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
 ---> Running in ee4e3eec0270
Removing intermediate container ee4e3eec0270
 ---> 53366cb4cafc
Step 3/3 : RUN echo $0
 ---> Running in 39bb8fd8ecae
/bin/bash
Removing intermediate container 39bb8fd8ecae
 ---> 1cbbe22fe598
Successfully built 1cbbe22fe598
```

And, as I've mentioned [earlier](https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1588#issuecomment-491616094), `RUN ["echo", "$0"]` doesn't work:
```
Step 3/3 : RUN ["echo", "$0"]
 ---> Running in ba2508344e4a
$0
Removing intermediate container ba2508344e4a
 ---> 5fe7ffc812ef
Successfully built 5fe7ffc812ef
```
I guess the bottom line then, does buildah bud work the same way with my patch?  Did my patch break anything, since it will allow `buildah run ...` to work the correct way.

I've tried to apply your patch, but I am getting really different results. Since the patch forces shell interpretation by prepending `sh`, `-c` to a parsed command, only the first argument seems to be passed to the shell. This seems to break multiple things:

- bud
```
STEP 1: FROM docker.io/ubuntu
STEP 2: RUN echo $0
/bin/sh
STEP 3: RUN ["echo", "$0"]

STEP 4: RUN ["apt-get", "install", "vim"]
apt 1.6.8 (amd64)
Usage: apt-get [options] command
...
^- echo and apt-get is called with no arguments
```

- run
```
$ buildah --format=docker from docker.io/ubuntu
ubuntu-working-container-2
$ buildah config --shell 'bash -c' ubuntu-working-container-2
$ buildah run ubuntu-working-container-2 sh -c 'echo $0'
# 
^- new bash shell is spawned since -c is dropped
```

Did you make any other changes apart from the ones on pastebin?
Ok lets merge your version now and look at the buildah run command separately so we can get this fix in.
LGTM
@TomSweeneyRedHat @giuseppe @vrothberg @nalind PTAL
@TomSweeneyRedHat @giuseppe @vrothberg @nalind PTAL
@rhatdan @EricHripko sorry, this one slipped off my stack, reviewing now.
Hold on the merge for a bit, ran into some issues testing that may be self induced, still poking.

Yep, self induced confusion.  
LGTM
